title: DC-area forecast: Humidity and a shower chance linger today. Nicer tomorrow into the weekend.
contentHtml:
Always a human at the helm: Updated around-the-clock by Capital Weather meteorologists.

Happening now: Partly sunny today with humidity slow to budge and highs in the mid-80s to near 90. A few showers or storms are possible this afternoon, especially from DC to the south and east.

What’s next? Partly to mostly sunny and pleasant Thursday through Saturday, with lower humidity and highs in the upper 70s to mid-80s.

Today’s daily digit — 6/10: Some periods of sun send the digit higher, even with stubborn humidity and a lingering shower chance. | 🤚 Your call?
The digit is a somewhat subjective rating of the day’s weather, on a 0-to-10 scale.

Forecast in detail

Today (Wednesday): Some sun at times today with an isolated shower possible through early afternoon. Highs reach the mid-80s to near 90 this afternoon as humidity remains moderate to high. A few showers or storms could pop up during the afternoon, especially from DC to the south and east. Winds come from the west around 10 mph. Confidence: Medium-High

Tonight: Still a shower or storm possible into the evening from near DC to the south and east. Humidity may start to come down a bit during the evening. Light winds from the north should drop humidity more significantly overnight. Lows settle in the low to mid-60s under partly to mostly clear skies. Confidence: High

Hourly Forecast | Pollen, Air & UV

Tomorrow (Thursday): About as nice as can be for the latter part of July. Mostly sunny with highs in the upper 70s to mid-80s and low humidity (dew points in the 50s). Confidence: High 😎 Nice Day!

Tomorrow night: Mostly clear and comfortable – not a word you hear often in July! Lows range from the upper 50s to low 60s. Confidence: High

📬 Get our forecast in your inbox

Our morning forecast email is back! It's included with every level of support — from Free to Sustainer — delivered 7 days a week. Sign up at: capitalweather.com/support-us

A look ahead

Friday and Saturday look like a pair of nice days. Both should be partly to mostly sunny with highs in the upper 70s to mid-80s and not too humid (dew points in the mid-50s to low 60s). Friday and Saturday night lows fall back to near 60 to the mid-60s. Confidence: Medium-High

Sunday looks like a decent day, too, with highs in the 80s. But perhaps with rising humidity and the chance of an isolated late-day shower or storm. Confidence: Low-Medium

The public preview period for our Weather Now, Forecast Lab, and Climate Room pages will come to an end this weekend, after which much of the content on those pages will be available exclusively to Friends, Founders, and Sustainers. Become a member to support our work and enjoy unlimited access to our premium content.

DC weather history for July 22

A day of extremes, from deadly record heat in 1926 to major flooding rains in 1969 and even a tornado damaging aircraft in 1987.
